title: Greenzo

jokes.text: This is sunset blush. I wear tiger orgasm.
jokes.setup: Normal conversation about finding lipstick that isn't hers
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurdly named makeup shade 'tiger orgasm'
jokes.explanation: Strong 30 Rock-style absurdist humor - mundane situation escalated with ridiculously named product. Very quotable and economical.

jokes.text: Pete's having an affair... The sexy justin timberlake hat.
jokes.setup: Evidence that Pete is having an affair
jokes.punchline: Among the evidence is a 'sexy Justin Timberlake hat'
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about middle-aged male behavior and pop culture fashion choices.

jokes.text: The only reason men start taking care of themselves is if they're getting someone to have sex with them. If it wasn't for that, they'd just sit at home in their own filth.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Pete must be having an affair
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about male hygiene motivation
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz Lemon cynical observation about relationships. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, Liz. We're out of string cheese, so I think I'm gonna take off.
jokes.setup: Pete appearing right after discussion of men sitting in their own filth
jokes.punchline: Pete demonstrates exactly this behavior - leaving work over string cheese
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - Pete immediately demonstrates the exact pathetic male behavior Liz just described.

jokes.text: To save the earth? So we can drain the remainder of its resources.
jokes.setup: Liz assumes environmental initiative is to save earth
jokes.punchline: Jack reveals it's actually to exploit it more efficiently
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack Donaghy moment - perfectly subverts expectations about corporate environmentalism with character-perfect corporate cynicism.

jokes.text: Greenzo! Saving the earth while maintaining profitability.
jokes.setup: Jack needs an environmental spokesperson
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous superhero name and oxymoronic slogan
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central absurdist premise with typical 30 Rock corporate doublespeak.

jokes.text: America's first non-judgmental, business-friendly environmental advocate. The free market will solve global warming, if that even exists.
jokes.setup: Jack describing Greenzo's mission
jokes.punchline: Casual climate change denial as corporate strategy
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate environmental messaging and climate denial packaged as moderate position.

jokes.text: Greenzo? Is that the first name that came to your head? Can you believe it? I mean, it just popped right there.
jokes.setup: Liz questions the obviousness of the name
jokes.punchline: Jack is proud of his uncreative naming
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about lazy creative processes, with Jack's oblivious pride being the real punchline.

jokes.text: My last real job was an ad for Rick Lazio's New York Senate campaign. 'Hillary Clinton wants an all-homosexual army. How will that affect my family?'
jokes.setup: Jared mentions his last acting job
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurdly homophobic political ad content
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire that captures the absurdity of fear-mongering campaign ads. Historically grounded but absurdist.

jokes.text: I just wish my mom were still alive so I could rub it in her fat face.
jokes.setup: Jared excited about being on the Today Show
jokes.punchline: Dark turn about his dead mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - suddenly reveals character's unresolved family issues in inappropriately petty terms.

jokes.text: I'm always the only person who shows up.
jokes.setup: Liz hiding from Kenneth's party invitation
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's the only one who ever shows up to his parties
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's loyalty despite social awkwardness, sets up episode plot.

jokes.text: Harvey Lemmings, my lawyer, who never misses a party. That's not a real person. You made that up.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining party psychology using his lawyer as example
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately realizes it's a made-up pun name
jokes.explanation: Clever pun on 'lemmings' behavior that Tracy tries to pass off as real, caught by Liz.

jokes.text: 'Cause if the earth's not here, where else is Greenzo gonna dance?
jokes.setup: Greenzo giving environmental advice
jokes.punchline: Self-centered reasoning for saving the planet
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Greenzo's narcissistic approach to environmentalism.

jokes.text: Sorry, everybody, that's Meredith Vieira.
jokes.setup: Greenzo getting praise from TV host
jokes.punchline: Corrects everyone on the host's name in self-important way
jokes.explanation: Shows Greenzo's growing ego and need to be seen as insider who knows celebrities personally.

jokes.text: It's wry and wise, but also very sexual.
jokes.setup: Jared describing Greenzo's voice for writing
jokes.punchline: Adds 'very sexual' to describe environmental character
jokes.explanation: Absurd character note that reveals actor's delusions about the role's depth and appeal.

jokes.text: Colored people, broads, fairies, commies. Gosh, we gotta update these forms.
jokes.setup: Jack reading demographic data about Greenzo
jokes.punchline: Reveals NBC's hilariously outdated demographic categories
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical jab at both corporate demographic thinking and historical broadcast industry attitudes. Perfect 30 Rock corporate absurdism.

jokes.text: Wow, if you licked the envelope, you could clone him, and then you'd have two Geisses.
jokes.setup: Jack excited about Don Geiss's personal signature
jokes.punchline: Liz's absurd cloning suggestion with plural wordplay
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz Lemon absurdist leap plus clever plural of 'Geiss' - 'Geisses'.

jokes.text: Yeah, right, Lemon. I'm gonna clone Geiss, then compete with a Geiss clone for the CEO position?
jokes.setup: Liz's absurd cloning suggestion
jokes.punchline: Jack takes it seriously and explains why it's strategically flawed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack response - treats absurd scenario with serious corporate strategy thinking.

jokes.text: I'm just a simple pig farmer's son.
jokes.setup: Kenneth about to make a request
jokes.punchline: Humble self-description with rural specificity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - his specific rural background and humility.

jokes.text: During your party, I'll be home, listening to some Schubert. And, uh, ironically, viewing some Canadian pornography.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he can't come to Kenneth's party
jokes.punchline: Combines highbrow classical music with oddly specific porn preference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack Donaghy character moment - combines pretentious high culture with weirdly specific low culture.

jokes.text: Hope you're ready to party like it's 1999. Which, according to my Bible, will be in seven years.
jokes.setup: Kenneth using Prince's party phrase
jokes.punchline: Reveals his Bible has different dating system
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kenneth moment - his innocent use of pop culture phrase reveals his alternative biblical chronology.

jokes.text: Your beautiful wife Paula. And your sons, Robert and Jack. And that creepy little one who's always rubbing himself against the carpet.
jokes.setup: Liz reminding Pete about his family
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal family to disturbing child behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure that goes from sweet family description to disturbing detail.

jokes.text: Oh, great, now I smell like midlife crisis!
jokes.setup: Pete's cologne/body spray affecting Liz
jokes.punchline: Describes the smell as embodying midlife crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant conceptual joke - that you could literally smell like a life stage/psychological condition.

jokes.text: Here's a tip, Cerie. Decide what you want before you open the refrigerator. You just released enough hydrofluorocarbons to kill a penguin. This penguin.
jokes.setup: Greenzo giving environmental advice about refrigerators
jokes.punchline: Escalates to specific penguin death, presumably shows picture
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from general advice to specific guilt trip with visual component.

jokes.text: You ever take off that costume? You ever take off yours? Greenzo out!
jokes.setup: Cerie questions if Greenzo ever removes his costume
jokes.punchline: Greenzo implies her appearance is also a costume
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that's both insulting and philosophically interesting about personas and authenticity.

jokes.text: Did he just talk to me like I'm ugly?
jokes.setup: Greenzo's costume/persona comeback
jokes.punchline: Cerie's confused reaction to being treated normally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cerie character moment - her confusion at not receiving special treatment for her looks.

jokes.text: Ken went to high school in Georgia with the rapper T.I. And, boys... T.I. might show up.
jokes.setup: Tracy creating rumor about Kenneth's party
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim that Kenneth knows T.I. from high school
jokes.explanation: Absurd but plausible-sounding lie that plays on Kenneth's mysterious background.

jokes.text: Bob Logan from Texas Instruments is gonna be there? Logan must be in contention for the CEO spot as well.
jokes.setup: Discussion of T.I. at the party
jokes.punchline: Jack misinterprets T.I. as business executive from tech company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection joke that perfectly captures Jack's corporate mindset - he can only process information through business lens.

jokes.text: Hey, you know what else takes a long time? Building a new earth.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about computer boot time
jokes.punchline: Greenzo connects everything to environmental messaging
jokes.explanation: Shows Greenzo's growing obsession - he can only see everything through environmental lens.

jokes.text: Do you even bother to compost your own feces?
jokes.setup: Greenzo lecturing about environmental hypocrisy
jokes.punchline: Escalates to extremely personal and gross suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Greenzo's environmental extremism to absurd personal territory.

jokes.text: Are you saying actors can't change the world? I guess nobody bothered to tell Sharon Stone.
jokes.setup: Liz dismissing actors changing the world
jokes.punchline: Greenzo cites Sharon Stone as counterexample
jokes.explanation: Decent celebrity reference joke, though relies somewhat on pop culture knowledge.

jokes.text: So let's have a little less yappity yap and a little more clackity clack.
jokes.setup: Greenzo demanding action over talk
jokes.punchline: Uses childish rhyming words to make serious point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Greenzo's pseudo-profound style with silly language.

jokes.text: You jag! I just got this, like, eight years ago.
jokes.setup: Greenzo breaks Liz's coffee mug
jokes.punchline: Liz's attachment to old, cheap mug
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment - her attachment to mundane possessions and economic lifestyle.

jokes.text: You're cheating with your wife?
jokes.setup: Liz catches Pete with Paula
jokes.punchline: Confused phrasing about cheating with rather than on
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures the absurdity of the situation through confused phrasing.

jokes.text: That's my fault. I get off on the sneakin' around. Who knew, right?
jokes.setup: Pete's wife explaining their secretive behavior
jokes.punchline: Reveals she enjoys the illicit thrill of sneaking around with her own husband
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing how married couple tries to recreate dating excitement.

jokes.text: Why is there a pop-tart in the bed? What do you do with the pop-tart?
jokes.setup: Discovery of food in intimate setting
jokes.punchline: Liz's horrified curiosity about sexual food use
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier pop-tart mention with added sexual implication and Liz's disturbed reaction.

jokes.text: It combines my two favorite things: Boxing and referees!
jokes.setup: Tracy excited about foxy boxing
jokes.punchline: Unexpected focus on referees rather than attractive women
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up expectation about male gaze but reveals unexpected fetish for authority figures.

jokes.text: You dummy, first of all, you didn't dial that cell phone. Second of all, that is your own rumor.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to call his lawyer about the party
jokes.punchline: Liz points out he's both not actually calling and believing his own lie
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about Tracy falling victim to his own rumor campaign.

jokes.text: Just like Colonial Williamsburg.
jokes.setup: Liz warning about disappointed party guests
jokes.punchline: Compares to historical tourist attraction disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about tourist trap disappointment used as comparison.

jokes.text: I'm just horny to be in New York
jokes.setup: Kenneth's rural cousin expressing excitement
jokes.punchline: Uses 'horny' instead of appropriate word like 'excited'
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism humor from Kenneth's rural family background.

jokes.text: Isn't that a fantastic joke?
jokes.setup: Jack claiming his party attendance reversal was a joke
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asking for validation of his non-joke
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jack's social awkwardness when trying to manipulate Kenneth.

jokes.text: So that's the way you wanna play this? Whip 'em out, measure 'em.
jokes.setup: Kenneth not immediately giving Jack what he wants
jokes.punchline: Jack treats party invitation as power struggle requiring penis comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack character moment - his corporate masculinity applied inappropriately to social situation.

jokes.text: Hey, kids, can you hold your breath? Good. Now do it forever. 'Cause the air is gonna be poisoned unless we switch to green technology. Sold by our company.
jokes.setup: Greenzo giving environmental advice to children
jokes.punchline: Tells kids to suffocate themselves, then reveals corporate sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that escalates from child endangerment to corporate sales pitch. Perfect satire of fear-based marketing.

jokes.text: It's rude to interrupt people.
jokes.setup: TV host trying to engage with Greenzo
jokes.punchline: Greenzo scolds professional interviewer for doing her job
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Greenzo's growing delusions and lack of media training.

jokes.text: I'm talking about big companies and their two-faced, fat cat executives.
jokes.setup: Greenzo criticizing corporate greed on environmental show
jokes.punchline: Attacks executives while literally employed by and representing GE
jokes.explanation: Great irony - attacking the very executives who hired him while on their network

jokes.text: Did you kids know that we could actually end global warming in five years, if we just raised taxes on the super-rich by just 2%?
jokes.setup: Greenzo supposedly giving environmental education
jokes.punchline: Turns into specific political policy proposal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Greenzo going completely off-script with radical political messaging.

jokes.text: It doesn't matter if it's true. He works for that money.
jokes.setup: TV host asking if Greenzo's claim is true
jokes.punchline: Jack's corporate response prioritizing wealth over truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - his corporate mindset that dismisses truth in favor of defending wealth.

jokes.text: So much hair.
jokes.setup: Pete apologizing for what happened in Liz's bed
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary detail about hair left behind
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor through unnecessary oversharing about bodily details.

jokes.text: We did it on your kitchen table. I shouldn't have told you that.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his new relationship with his wife
jokes.punchline: Immediately overshares about sex location, then realizes mistake
jokes.explanation: Good escalating overshare with immediate self-awareness of the mistake.

jokes.text: If there were money in the future instead of just hugs.
jokes.setup: Greenzo saying he'll be on future money
jokes.punchline: Reveals his vision of future economy based on hugs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - Greenzo's utopian vision of post-monetary hug economy.

jokes.text: Can you fire the wind, Jack? Can you fire a hurricane?
jokes.setup: Jack firing Greenzo
jokes.punchline: Greenzo compares himself to unstoppable natural forces
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Greenzo's delusions of grandeur - comparing himself to natural disasters.

jokes.text: We're developing that technology!
jokes.setup: Greenzo asking if Jack can fire natural forces
jokes.punchline: Jack's corporate response that GE is working on controlling weather
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack line - perfectly captures his corporate mindset that believes any problem can be solved through technology and capitalism.

jokes.text: Y'all should be ashamed of yourselves. Mr. Lutz, you ate all my parakeet's medication.
jokes.setup: Kenneth scolding party guests for bad behavior
jokes.punchline: Specific accusation about eating bird medication
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - very specific and bizarre party misconduct that could only happen in 30 Rock universe.

jokes.text: And thanks to you, Sonny Crockett has been having seizures all morning.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining consequences of eaten bird medication
jokes.punchline: Reveals parakeet is named after Miami Vice character and is now sick
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth character detail - his parakeet named after 80s TV character, plus real consequences of absurd party behavior.

jokes.text: Mr. Jordan, I saw you steal my sink.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing party crimes
jokes.punchline: Tracy stole an entire bathroom fixture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy behavior - stealing large household fixtures fits his character's entitled celebrity absurdity.

jokes.text: Mr. Jordan, I saw you steal my sink.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing party damages
jokes.punchline: Tracy literally stole a sink from Kenneth's apartment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy behavior - stealing large plumbing fixtures from host's home, absurd but believable

jokes.text: Harlem Globetrotter... does that name mean nothing to you?
jokes.setup: Kenneth addressing someone's bad behavior
jokes.punchline: Appeals to Harlem Globetrotters' reputation for good sportsmanship
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier rumor, with Kenneth's innocent belief in basketball team's moral authority.

jokes.text: I will have you know that before last night, I had never, ever seen Grizz or Dotcom cry.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing party aftermath
jokes.punchline: The tough guys were reduced to tears by party disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the emotional impact on typically stoic characters.

jokes.text: You were all present at Kenneth parcell's last party ever.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's disappointment with party aftermath
jokes.punchline: Dramatically declares he'll never host another party
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic Kenneth moment - treating party failure like historical tragedy

jokes.text: Is he gonna be the new Greenzo? Uh, not exactly. I may have gotten him here under false pretenses.
jokes.setup: Liz seeing Al Gore, assuming he's replacing Greenzo
jokes.punchline: Jack admits to lying to former Vice President
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jack's corporate ruthlessness extending to deceiving political figures.

jokes.text: We met when Jack was an intern for Senator Ted Kennedy. Let me tell ya, Jack was the most liberal guy--
jokes.setup: Al Gore about to reveal Jack's political past
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jack was extremely liberal, contradicting his current corporate conservatism
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Jack's political evolution/sellout through real political figure's testimony.

jokes.text: Your parent company could lobby Congress and the president to pass the treaty and save the climate. Yes, or... you could put on a silly hat and tell the kids how outsourcing means cheaper toys at Christmas.
jokes.setup: Al Gore suggesting serious environmental action
jokes.punchline: Jack's alternative of using environmentalism to sell outsourcing to children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical contrast between genuine environmental action and corporate co-opting of environmental messaging for profit.

jokes.text: Back off, Jack! Or you'll be Greenzoed! You'll all be Greenzoed!
jokes.setup: Jared refusing to leave
jokes.punchline: Threatens people with being 'Greenzoed' as if it's a real threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Greenzo's delusions - treating his persona as having real power to threaten people.

jokes.text: A whale is in trouble. I have to go.
jokes.setup: Chaotic scene with Greenzo going crazy
jokes.punchline: Al Gore leaves due to unrelated whale emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist exit line that treats environmental emergencies as superseding all other concerns, very economical.

jokes.text: Oh, boy, okay. This earth is ruined! We gotta get a new one.
jokes.setup: All the environmental chaos and failure
jokes.punchline: Liz's defeatist conclusion that they should just get a new planet
jokes.explanation: Good episode-ending joke that captures the futility theme and Liz's practical defeatism.

tmdb_overview: It's time for Kenneth's annual house party, but he may be the only one excited about it. The soiree has only been attended by Liz in the past, so in order to get a better turnout Tracy spreads rumors that ultimately get out of hand.  Meanwhile, Jack deals with NBC's new environmentally-friendly mascot, whose new-found stardom goes to his head.
insight: Environmental satire escalates brilliantly—65 jokes in 22 minutes sustains absurdist pace throughout.
